profiles.known_skill_support_status: AGENTS.md-style repo guidance documented; external skill execution remains environment-dependent
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Use exact tool schemas when available; prefer compact contracts and evidence fields over long background dumps.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Call vnem_bootstrap, then vnem_compose_capability_contract for the real user task.
Use AGENTS.md/repo guidance when working in a checkout, but do not turn unrelated user work into VNEM self-improvement.
weaknesses: can over-read large docs if guidance is not scoped
avoid_instructions: Do not dump Claude-only setup steps into Codex tasks.
Do not use VNEM repo-development rules unless the task is explicitly VNEM development.
token_efficiency_tips: Return IDs/URIs for details instead of full library entries.

profiles.known_skill_support_status: Claude Code has MCP/plugin and instruction workflows; skill install behavior depends on configured environment
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Prefer MCP tools with small schemas and explicit read-only boundaries.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Use VNEM Core MCP as the read-only planning/capability layer.
Keep Precision/Tools MCP separate for mutation.
weaknesses: external MCP servers can introduce prompt-injection risk
may over-apply tool-specific instructions if not scoped
avoid_instructions: Do not connect unreviewed MCP servers or skills blindly.
Do not treat Core MCP guidance as permission to mutate files.
token_efficiency_tips: Use vnem_get_agent_profile only for Claude when Claude is the client.
Keep capability output compact and fetch details by id.

profiles.known_mcp_support_status: client-dependent; Gemini API documents function calling, not universal MCP-client parity
profiles.known_skill_support_status: unknown unless a specific Gemini client declares skill support
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Gemini function calling returns structured calls; the application executes functions and returns results.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Expose VNEM Core MCP concepts through function/tool declarations when MCP client support is unavailable.
Use compact JSON contracts and function-response evidence.
weaknesses: MCP behavior depends on the wrapper/client
avoid_instructions: Do not assume Claude/Codex MCP setup commands apply to Gemini.
Do not imply Gemini itself executes functions.
token_efficiency_tips: Return one profile and selected modules only.

profiles.known_mcp_support_status: unknown unless provided by the calling client/framework
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: DeepSeek documents OpenAI-compatible function calling; the model requests calls but the user/application executes functions.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Use VNEM contracts as compact function/tool-callable JSON guidance.
strengths: structured tool calls through API when configured
weaknesses: no assumed native MCP client behavior
avoid_instructions: Do not assume DeepSeek has Claude/Codex skill behavior.
Do not claim tools were executed by the model itself.
token_efficiency_tips: Use compact/normal budgets only unless user asks for expanded detail.

profiles.known_mcp_support_status: toolset/profile-dependent; this repo documents Hermes/VNEM workflows locally
profiles.known_skill_support_status: Hermes skill system exists in the local agent environment; external skills still require review
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Use tool calls for verification and keep VNEM Core MCP read-only.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Load relevant Hermes skills separately when operating Hermes itself.
Use VNEM Core MCP contracts to improve the user's task, not to self-optimize VNEM unless requested.
weaknesses: can over-focus on available repo context if task is not scoped
avoid_instructions: Do not conflate Hermes skills with unreviewed external skills.
Do not emit unrelated VNEM repo work for non-VNEM tasks.
token_efficiency_tips: Use compact contracts and only load deeper skill docs when needed.

profiles.known_mcp_support_status: framework/client-dependent; Qwen docs cover function/tool calling patterns
profiles.known_skill_support_status: unknown unless a wrapper implements skills
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Qwen docs describe tool/function calling with structured schemas; execution belongs to the application/framework.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Represent VNEM contracts as compact function/tool results.
Avoid client-specific MCP setup claims unless the actual wrapper supports them.
weaknesses: MCP/client behavior should not be assumed
template details can vary by serving stack
avoid_instructions: Do not feed Claude/Codex-specific install instructions unless relevant.
Do not imply automatic execution of tools.
token_efficiency_tips: Use compact selected modules; avoid dumping profiles.

profiles.known_skill_support_status: unknown unless the client declares skill support
profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Assume only the MCP tools exposed by the current client are callable; do not assume filesystem or terminal tools.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Call VNEM Core MCP read-only tools for routing/contracts.
Use Precision/Tools MCP only if separately configured and approved.
avoid_instructions: Do not assume high-power tools exist.
Do not install skills or call APIs from Core MCP.
token_efficiency_tips: Keep contracts compact and use IDs for lookup.

profiles.function_tool_calling_notes: Use generic schema/function-calling guidance only; do not assume MCP, skill, filesystem, terminal, or browser support.
recommended_vnem_usage_style: Return compact task contracts and evidence requirements.
Ask the host/client to execute any real-world action outside Core MCP.
weaknesses: actual tools and instruction hierarchy are unknown
avoid_instructions: Do not provide client-specific setup steps.
